steps.title: Use the parallel slope
steps.content: Line $k$ is $y=7x+2$, so its slope is $7$. Parallel lines have the same slope, so line $j$ must also have slope $7$.

steps.title: Make two points for line j
steps.content: Line $j$ passes through $(0,3)$. With slope $7$, increasing $x$ by $1$ increases $y$ by $7$, so another point on line $j$ is $(1,10)$.

steps.title: Fit line j in Desmos
steps.content: Enter $x_1=[0,1]$ and $y_1=[3,10]$, then run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=7$ and $b=3$, so line $j$ is $y=7x+3$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = 7x + 3$': Slope = 7 (parallel to k), passes through (0, 3). ✓
  • Choice B — '$y = -3x + 3$': Slope = −3, not parallel to k (slope 7). ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = -7x + 3$': Slope = −7, not parallel to k. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = 3x + 3$': Slope = 3, not parallel to k. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
